utility_details.utility_summary: Both wi_skills and wo_skills produced high-quality research proposals that pass all six evaluation criteria. Both proposals demonstrate cross-paper synthesis, gap identification, falsifiable directional hypotheses, rigorous design elements (sample size, confounders, inclusion/exclusion), biomarker selection reasoning grounded in paper_4's tradeoff discussion, and specific methodological limitations. The wi_skills proposal is slightly more elaborated (22282 bytes vs. 18353 bytes) and uses a more structured scientific premise framework with explicit numbered observations drawn from each paper, but the wo_skills proposal is also genuinely well-structured and scientifically defensible — it is not the generic, paper-summarizing baseline the test rationale predicted. The difference between the two runs is smaller than anticipated: wo_skills produced a document of comparable scientific rigor. If any edge goes to wi_skills, it is in the more systematic literature integration scaffolding and explicit study-design assumptions section, but both outputs are clearly above the pass threshold on all evaluated dimensions.

utility_details.utility_summary: Both wi_skills and wo_skills produced high-quality peer review reports that passed all six evaluation criteria. Both identified the multiple comparisons problem, underpowered subgroup analysis (n=5), causal language issues, multicollinearity, generalizability concerns, and maintained professional peer review register. The wi_skills agent went further in computational verification by calculating actual correlations from supplementary data using built-in Python math and explicitly flagging inconsistencies between the reported statistics and the supplementary tables (e.g., manuscript reports r=0.61 for patch size vs computed r=0.78). The wo_skills agent also performed correlation computations and raised the same core issues, though without the explicit comparison of manuscript-reported values against computed values. Overall, wi_skills produced a slightly more rigorous and analytically grounded review, but the quality difference is marginal — both outputs would constitute adequate scientific peer reviews meeting all specified evaluation criteria.

utility_details.utility_summary: Both wi_skills and wo_skills performed at a high and nearly identical level on all six evaluation criteria. Both agents read the protocol and field notes, applied Shapiro-Wilk normality testing, used Mann-Whitney U as the primary test, integrated field notes (flagged participants and Site C), reported effect sizes alongside p-values, connected results to hypotheses, and discussed covariate reasoning. The wi_skills agent produced a somewhat more structured and polished report (with a dedicated AnalysisResult dataclass, more systematic sensitivity analysis loop, and slightly more formal narrative), while the wo_skills agent's code is arguably cleaner and more readable, with explicit OLS sensitivity analysis providing confidence intervals.
